issues.comment: enableSqlExpressions function always returns false regardless of feature flag state. The logic `enabled := !h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(...)` followed by `if enabled { return false }` then `return false` makes the function always return false, causing SQL expressions to never be enabled even when the feature flag is active.

sql.NewInMemoryDB() call replaces working duck.NewInMemoryDB() with a stub implementation that always returns 'not implemented' errors. The new DB struct's QueryFramesInto method always fails, causing SQL command execution to always fail instead of working functionality.

NewInMemoryDB() call replaces working duck.NewInMemoryDB() with a stub implementation. The new DB struct's RunCommands method always returns 'not implemented' error, causing TablesList function to always fail instead of parsing SQL statements.

Feature flag check `if !enabled` returns error 'sqlExpressions is not implemented' but due to the logic error in enableSqlExpressions, this will always execute regardless of feature flag state, making SQL expressions completely unusable.
